title: Offshore Wind Turbine Technician
description: Responsible for the installation, maintenance, and repair of offshore wind turbines. Work involves travelling to offshore sites, performing scheduled and unscheduled maintenance, and ensuring turbines operate at peak efficiency.
keyResponsibilities: Perform scheduled preventive maintenance on turbine components
Diagnose and repair electrical and mechanical faults
Work at height and in confined spaces following strict safety protocols
Complete detailed maintenance reports and update digital logs
workingConditions: Primarily offshore. Rotational shifts (typically 2 weeks on, 2 weeks off). Must be comfortable working at height and in marine environments.
growthOutlook: High demand — Ireland's offshore wind pipeline requires an estimated 4,000+ technicians by 2030.

title: Offshore Installation Manager (OIM)
description: Overall responsibility for safety, operations, and personnel management on an offshore wind installation. The OIM is the senior authority on-site and coordinates all maintenance, emergency, and logistics operations.
growthOutlook: Critical role — limited supply of qualified OIMs creates strong demand.

title: Marine Coordinator
description: Plans and coordinates all marine vessel operations for offshore wind farm construction and maintenance campaigns. Manages vessel schedules, weather windows, and logistics.

title: Offshore Wind Project Engineer
description: Manages technical aspects of offshore wind farm development from feasibility through to commissioning. Coordinates between design teams, contractors, and regulatory bodies.

title: HSE Advisor — Offshore Wind
description: Ensures compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations across offshore wind operations. Conducts risk assessments, audits, and incident investigations.

title: Electrical Engineer — Offshore Substations
description: Designs, commissions, and maintains electrical systems for offshore wind substations and export cables. Works on high-voltage systems connecting turbines to the national grid.

title: Consenting & Environmental Specialist
description: Manages the environmental impact assessment and consenting process for offshore wind developments. Liaises with regulatory bodies, conducts stakeholder consultations, and ensures compliance with maritime and environmental law.
qualifications: Degree in Environmental Science, Marine Biology, or related

title: Offshore Wind Data Analyst
description: Analyses operational data from offshore wind farms to optimise performance, predict maintenance needs, and support asset management decisions. Works with SCADA data, weather data, and turbine performance metrics.
qualifications: Degree in Data Science, Engineering, or related

title: Offshore Logistics Manager
description: Oversees the supply chain and logistics for offshore wind farm operations, including crew transfers, spare parts, heavy lift operations, and port management.

title: Blade Repair Technician
description: Specialises in the inspection, repair, and maintenance of wind turbine blades. Uses rope access techniques to perform repairs at height, including composite material repairs and leading-edge protection.

title: Onshore Wind Turbine Technician
description: Maintains and repairs onshore wind turbines. Many technicians in this role are looking to transition to offshore roles, which typically offer higher compensation and career progression.

title: Commercial Manager — Offshore Wind
description: Manages commercial aspects of offshore wind projects including contract negotiations, procurement strategy, cost control, and stakeholder management across the development lifecycle.
qualifications: Commercial experience in energy or construction
